domain: Hausa zango (Sahel/West-Africa trans-Saharan trade-station system; walled lodging/trade-stations at oases Damergu, Katsina, Kano where caravans rested; each station accumulated cross-caravan news, commodity prices, route conditions; outbound caravans received digest of accumulated info plus freshness decay)
specific_mechanism: HAUSA-ZANGO: persistent-station multi-hop information cascade for long-horizon LLM agents. (1) Define M stations S_1,...,S_M with each S_i holding a token-buffer B_i(t) that accumulates messages from multiple agent threads. (2) Per-message exponential decay w_j(t) = exp(-(t-t_j)/τ_i) per-station τ. (3) Outbound digest D_i = top-K weighted summarization of B_i. (4) Agent A_n on traversing S_i: reads D_i, appends newly-discovered facts to B_i (with timestamp), continues to S_{i+1}. (5) Cross-station read paths: agent at S_i can query D_j for j≠i (sparse station-graph). (6) Station-graph topology learned or fixed (e.g., zango-graph of major oasis cities).
llm_application: HAUSA-ZANGO: persistent-station context-relay for long-horizon LLM agents with cross-agent message accumulation. Differs from CHAP NDSS 2026 (single-agent shift handoff with compressed state — not multi-agent accumulation, not persistent station-graph), Context Folding (single-agent sub-trajectory branch+fold — not persistent multi-agent station accumulator), Multi-Agent Protocol 2510.17149 (compares protocols incl. cascade but no persistent-station decay buffer), Chain of Agents NeurIPS 2024 (serial single-thread handoff — not persistent stations across multiple threads). Differs from R422 CHASQUI-RELAY (individual runner handoff, not station accumulator), R429 KAMI-SHIBAI (bordered-panel serial narrative, not station), R438 PIROGUE-CASCADE (segment-payload not station accumulator) by EXPLICIT PERSISTENT MULTI-AGENT STATION BUFFER + DECAY + STATION-GRAPH READ-PATHS.
novelty_claim: CHAP + Context Folding + Multi-Agent Protocol + Chain of Agents cover agent handoff + cascade. HAUSA-ZANGO adds (a) persistent station-buffer accumulating across multiple agent threads + (b) per-station exponential decay τ + (c) sparse station-graph cross-station read paths. Recombination — buffer + decay + graph are mainstream.
